intro: The core problem lies in the absence of a unified, intelligent, and adaptive smart museum system that can simultaneously address personalization, accessibility, navigation, and feedback. Existing solutions tend to focus on individual components rather than delivering a comprehensive platform that integrates these functionalities.

gaps.title: Lack of Unified Personalization
gaps.description: Existing museum systems lack role-based content personalization that adapts artifact descriptions for different visitor categories such as tourists, students, teachers, professors, and citizens.

gaps.title: Limited Accessibility and Multilingual Support
gaps.description: Current solutions provide insufficient multilingual support and do not leverage NLP-based APIs for real-time translation of artifact descriptions to accommodate diverse international visitors.

gaps.title: Absent Adaptive Indoor Navigation
gaps.description: Traditional museums lack intelligent indoor navigation systems using BLE beacon positioning, sensor data, and GIS-based mapping for dynamic and context-aware route optimization inside the museum.

gaps.title: No Emotion-Aware Feedback Mechanism
gaps.description: Existing platforms do not capture facial and vocal expressions to analyze visitor emotions or provide AI-driven multilingual conversational support through chatbot systems.

gaps.title: Fragmented Visualization Solutions
gaps.description: There is no integrated interactive 2D and 3D artifact visualization system using deep learning-based reconstruction and generative models, leaving visitors without immersive artifact exploration experiences.
